title: feat: support AI-generated masks in shape_to_mask
body: Updated the export functionality to support AI-generated masks. The function now handles mask data from JSON files, including AI-generated mask formats (list/array and base64 encoded images).

step_plan.summary: Add support for AI-generated masks by updating shape_to_mask to accept mask arrays and base64 mask strings and by updating shapes_to_label to prefer per-shape 'mask' fields (list/array or base64) before rasterizing vector shapes. This allows export_json and other callers of shapes_to_label to produce correct labels when shapes include embedded masks.

steps.operation: Extend shape_to_mask to accept precomputed masks and base64 mask strings before rasterizing vector geometry
steps.reason: Centralize mask decoding/acceptance so callers can pass either vector geometry or mask-like inputs (numpy array / nested list / base64-encoded image). This enables code paths that previously assumed points-only to accept AI-generated masks without changing all callers.
steps.side_effects: All callers of shape_to_mask (including shapes_to_label and any other utilities) will now accept mask arrays and base64 strings. New imports (numpy as np and utils.img_b64_to_arr) and logging will be required. Behavior must remain backward-compatible for vector shapes (same rasterization path). Potential performance impact when decoding base64 images. Need unit tests for: ndarray/list masks, base64 masks with/without alpha channel, and shape mismatch handling. If mask shape mismatches image shape, the function should log a warning and return an all-False mask (or otherwise safe fallback) rather than raising, to avoid breaking export flows; downstream logic that expects masks may need to handle empty masks.

steps.operation: Modify shapes_to_label to prefer and consume a per-shape 'mask' field (list/array or base64 string) before falling back to rasterization
steps.reason: export_json and other code paths that pass raw JSON shapes must be able to interpret AI-generated masks embedded in the shape dict (shape['mask']) so label images produced by shapes_to_label reflect those masks. Checking shape['mask'] first ensures LabelFile.load behavior (which already decodes masks) and raw JSON flows both work consistently.
steps.side_effects: Export behavior will change when shapes include a 'mask' key — masks encoded as nested lists or base64 strings will be used directly. Must validate mask dimension matches image size; if not, log and skip/ignore the mask for the shape to avoid exceptions. This change affects outputs produced by export_json and any other code that calls shapes_to_label with raw shape dicts — tests relying on older behavior should be updated. Keep class/instance assignment semantics the same (cls[mask] = cls_id / ins[mask] = ins_id). Ensure no API signature changes are introduced. Also add/adjust unit tests covering mask decoding, alpha-channel handling, and shape compatibility.
